# UC-22.61.12 · CSL Art. 21 + DSL Art. 27 + GB/T 22239-2019 6-month security-log retention + integrity-protected archival + tamper-detection

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Intermediate &middot; **Pillar:** Security &middot; **Type:** Compliance, Audit &middot; **Wave:** Crawl &middot; **Status:** Verified

*Chinese law says important security records must be kept for at least 6 months and protected from being changed or deleted. We check every day that all our security logs are kept long enough and have not been tampered with.*

---

## Description

Reconciles every MLPS-graded log index against three operational limbs of CSL Art. 21(3): (i) the 180-day retention floor (configured `frozenTimePeriodInSecs`), (ii) the integrity-hash freshness (a hash-chain verification job must have run within 7 days and returned `valid`), and (iii) audit-trail tamper signals (any `_audit` index deletion or modification against the protected log indexes). Risk-scores invalid integrity chains at 100 (potential evidence destruction), tamper signals on L3+ at 95, retention breaches on L3+ at 90.

## Value

CSL Art. 21(3) is the CSL clause most frequently cited in MPS administrative-warning notices. A common pattern is: an incident occurs, the network operator is unable to produce 6 months of authentication logs, MPS issues an administrative warning under Art. 59 and may escalate to ordered remediation. Detecting retention breaches and integrity-chain invalidations before an incident is the difference between a routine MLPS finding and a public administrative warning.

## Implementation

(1) Curate `cn_csl_log_retention_lookup` from the MLPS register — every Level 1-5 system maps to one or more Splunk indexes. (2) Schedule a daily hash-chain verification job that signs the previous day's bucket manifest with the org's PRC-resident KMS-backed key and writes the result to `hec:integrity:hash:freshness`. (3) Schedule UC-22.61.12 daily at 03:00 Beijing time; route `risk_score = 100` (invalid hash chain) to CISO at severity P1.

## Evidence

Saved search `UC-22.61.12` archived daily to `audit_evidence` with `cn_csl_clause="CSL-Art-21"`. Every row pins (mlps_system_id, log_index_or_source, earliest_retained_event, oldest_retained_event, retention_days, integrity_hash_freshness, tamper_events_24h, risk_score).

## Control test

### Positive scenario

Configure a Level 3 log index with frozenTimePeriodInSecs=14515200 (168 days, 12 days short of the 180-day floor). UC-22.61.12 MUST flag with risk_score >= 90.

### Negative scenario

Configure the same index with frozenTimePeriodInSecs=15897600 (184 days), integrity hashes refreshed daily, and zero `_audit` index tamper events. UC-22.61.12 MUST record risk_score=0.

## Detailed Implementation

## Prerequisites

- Splunk Enterprise >= 9.2 or Splunk Cloud (PRC region).
- UC-22.61.1 deployed with `cn_mlps_register_lookup` populated.
- PRC-resident KMS-backed signing key for hash-chain verification (often a Huawei OBS / Alibaba KMS instance with a key registered to the legal entity).

## Step 1 - Retention KV Store

```ini
[cn_csl_log_retention_lookup]
enforceTypes = true
field.system_id = string
field.mlps_grade = string
field.log_index = string
field.expected_retention_days = number
field.integrity_scheme = string
field.hash_chain_endpoint = string
field.archive_handoff_documented = bool
field.cold_archive_evidence_ref = string
field.index_created_at = time
```

## Step 2 - Hash-chain verification job

A daily scheduled report uses `dbinspect` to enumerate bucket IDs + earliest/latest event times for every log_index in the lookup; the report writes a signed manifest to `hec:integrity:hash:freshness` with `hash_chain_status` set to `valid` only if every bucket's hash signature verifies against the PRC-resident KMS key.

## Step 3 - Schedule

```ini
[UC-22.61.12: CSL Art. 21 Log Retention]
cron_schedule = 0 3 * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -1d@d
dispatch.latest_time = now
actions = summary_index, notable
action.summary_index._name = audit_evidence
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.61.12,reg=CN-CSL,clause=CSL-Art-21
alert.severity = 4
```

## Step 4 - PRC-specific notes

- **Cross-border audit-data export**: even routine MLPS-3+ log review by an offshore SOC may trigger CSL Art. 37 + PIPL Art. 38 cross-border-transfer considerations if the logs contain PI — coordinate with UC-22.61.4 (egress detector) and UC-22.61.7 (cross-border transfer register).
- **Public-Security Bureau evidence-request mode**: in active criminal investigations, MPS may request raw logs under Art. 28; the chain-of-custody report from this UC is the artefact most commonly tendered.

## SPL

```spl
| inputlookup cn_csl_log_retention_lookup
| eval now_ts = now()
| eval expected_retention_days = coalesce(expected_retention_days, 180)
| eval required_floor_days = max(180, expected_retention_days)
| join type=left log_index [
    | rest /services/data/indexes
    | rename title as log_index
    | eval configured_retention_days = round(frozenTimePeriodInSecs / 86400, 1)
    | table log_index configured_retention_days frozenTimePeriodInSecs
  ]
| join type=left log_index [
    search index=_internal earliest=-7d sourcetype=splunkd component=BucketMover (action=freeze OR "freezing bucket")
    | stats max(_time) as last_bucket_frozen_ts by idx
    | rename idx as log_index
  ]
| join type=left log_index [
    search index=summary source=hec:integrity:hash:freshness earliest=-7d
    | stats max(_time) as last_hash_chain_verified_ts, latest(hash_chain_status) as hash_chain_status by log_index
  ]
| join type=left log_index [
    search index=_audit earliest=-7d (action=index_delete OR action=index_modify OR operation=delete)
    | stats count as tamper_events_7d, latest(user) as tamper_user by idx
    | rename idx as log_index
  ]
| eval tamper_events_7d = coalesce(tamper_events_7d, 0)
| eval days_since_hash_verified = round((now_ts - last_hash_chain_verified_ts) / 86400, 1)
| eval retention_breach = if(configured_retention_days < required_floor_days, 1, 0)
| eval integrity_stale = if(days_since_hash_verified > 7 OR isnull(last_hash_chain_verified_ts), 1, 0)
| eval integrity_invalid = if(hash_chain_status = "invalid" OR hash_chain_status = "failed", 1, 0)
| eval tamper_signal = if(tamper_events_7d > 0, 1, 0)
| eval risk_score = case(
    integrity_invalid = 1, 100,
    tamper_signal = 1 AND mlps_grade IN ("L3","L4","L5"), 95,
    retention_breach = 1 AND mlps_grade IN ("L3","L4","L5"), 90,
    retention_breach = 1, 70,
    integrity_stale = 1 AND mlps_grade IN ("L3","L4","L5"), 65,
    integrity_stale = 1, 40,
    1=1, 0)
| where risk_score > 0
| sort 0 -risk_score, +configured_retention_days
| table system_id mlps_grade log_index configured_retention_days required_floor_days integrity_scheme days_since_hash_verified hash_chain_status tamper_events_7d tamper_user risk_score
```

## Visualization

(Panel 1) Retention-vs-floor scatter (configured days vs MLPS grade). (Panel 2) Hash-chain freshness heat map. (Panel 3) Tamper-event timeline. (Panel 4) Retention-breach trend (90 days).

## Known False Positives

1. **Newly-created index**: an index created in the last 30 days has no aged data and may report `configured_retention_days = 180` correctly but `oldest_retained_event` < 180 days simply because no bucket has aged that far yet — load `index_created_at` and exclude from the retention-breach alert during the first 180 days of the index's life. 2. **Frozen-to-glacier handoff**: if frozen buckets are migrated to a cold archive (e.g., S3 Glacier / OBS Archive) the in-Splunk `frozenTimePeriodInSecs` may be set lower deliberately — load `archive_handoff_documented = true` and pass `cold_archive_evidence_ref` to suppress. 3. **Test/dev index** that genuinely doesn't carry production data should be marked `system_id = test_only` and excluded.

**Suppression mechanism**: KV Store `cn_csl_22_61_12_exceptions` with the CISO documented approval, an explicit system_id + log_index scope, an expiry date, and a written justification.

## References

- [Cybersecurity Law of the People's Republic of China — Article 21](http://www.npc.gov.cn/npc/c30834/201611/270b43e8b35e4f7ea98502b6f0e26f8a.shtml)
- [GB/T 22239-2019 §7.1.4.7 — Audit-trail tamper-resistance and retention](https://www.tc260.org.cn/upload/2019-05-13/1557715032893055115.pdf)
- [Data Security Law of the People's Republic of China — Article 29](http://www.npc.gov.cn/npc/c30834/202106/7c9af12f51334a73b56d7938f99a788a.shtml)
